title: brush_fire
description: A brush fire ignites near a populated trailhead and spreads rapidly toward nearby structures.

timeline.title: Fire Spotted
timeline.description: Hikers report smoke rising from dense brush near the trailhead. Confirm fire location.

timeline.title: Rapid Spread
timeline.description: Fire grows quickly due to dry vegetation. Warn nearby hikers and prepare evacuation.

timeline.title: Air Support Requested
timeline.description: Ground crews unable to contain spread. Request aerial retardant drop.

timeline.title: Perimeter Established
timeline.description: Fire crews establish a control line. Monitor for spot fires beyond the perimeter.

timeline.title: Structures Threatened
timeline.description: Fire pushes toward a nearby ranger station. Prepare defensive operations.

timeline.title: Fire Contained
timeline.description: Control lines hold. Begin mop-up operations and assess structural damage.

node-1.title: Brush Fire Reported
node-1.situation: Hikers at a popular trailhead report smoke rising from dry brush. Temperatures are high and winds are gusty. The fire appears small but growing.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Alert dispatch, request fire crews, and begin evacuating the trailhead area.

options.label: B
options.text: Send a single ranger to investigate before calling for additional resources.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait to see if the fire self-extinguishes given the location.

node-2.title: Evacuation Underway
node-2.situation: Hikers are being evacuated. The fire has spread significantly and is now moving toward a cluster of maintenance structures. Air support has been requested.
node-2.question: How do you prioritize resources?

options.label: A
options.text: Focus ground crews on structure protection while air support targets the fire front.

options.label: B
options.text: Pull all crews back and rely entirely on aerial operations.

options.label: C
options.text: Commit all ground crews to direct attack on the fire front, ignoring structures.

node-3.title: Control Line Decision
node-3.situation: Ground crews have identified a natural firebreak along a dirt road. Establishing a control line here would protect the structures but leave some vegetation to burn.
node-3.question: What do you order?

options.label: A
options.text: Use the dirt road as a control line and prepare the structures with sprinklers and cleared brush.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to cut a hand line directly through the burning brush to stop the fire sooner.

node-4.title: Spot Fire Detected
node-4.situation: A spot fire has jumped the control line and is igniting new brush 50 meters away. Crews are fatigued and resources are stretched.
node-4.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Redirect a crew to attack the spot fire immediately and request additional resources.

options.label: B
options.text: Ignore the spot fire and focus all resources on holding the main control line.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Both the main fire and spot fire have been contained. Structures are intact and all personnel are safe. Mop-up operations are underway.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
